name: Comarca de la Jara – circular to Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya
name_original: Comarca de la Jara – circular a Sierra Ancha y Sierra Aguda desde Aldeanueva de Barbarroya
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and significant elevation changes, particularly towards the Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda. The region features a mix of open countryside and areas with natural vegetation, offering varied terrain for road cyclists. Routes often include ascents and descents, providing a challenging environment for those seeking elevation gain. Best road cycling routes around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya The most popular road cycling route is Comarca de la Jara – circular to Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya, a 39.6 miles (63.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular path through the Comarca de la Jara, featuring views of the Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Robledo del Mazo – Los Navalucillos loop from Estación de Silos, a difficult 84.4 miles (135.9 km) path. This route includes substantial elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced road cyclists seeking a demanding ride through the region's varied topography. Local road cyclists also love the Pico Villuercas Summit – Puerto de Arrebatachacas loop from Estación de Silos, a 120.7 miles (194.2 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant climbs, often completed in about 10 hours 35 minutes. Road cycling around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya is defined by its rolling hills, mountain passes, and open rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult routes with considerable elevation changes. The routes in Aldeanueva De Barbarroya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 110 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Aldeanueva De Barbarroya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya?
A: There are 10 road cycling routes documented around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of road cycling routes in this region?
A: The road cycling routes around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya are generally challenging. There are 3 moderate routes and 7 difficult routes, with no easy options, indicating a landscape suited for cyclists seeking significant elevation changes and demanding rides.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Given the terrain characterized by rolling hills and significant elevation changes, most routes around Aldeanueva De Barbarroya are classified as moderate to difficult. There are no easy routes, so options for beginners or families with young children may be limited.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for road cycling routes here?
A: Routes vary significantly in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025309331/comarca-de-la-jara-circular-to-sierra-ancha-and-sierra-aguda-from-aldeanueva-de-barbarroya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca de la Jara – circular to Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya&lt;/a&gt; is about 39.6 miles (63.8 km) and takes nearly 3 hours, while the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31866479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Villuercas Summit – Puerto de Arrebatachacas loop from Estación de Silos&lt;/a&gt; spans 120.7 miles (194.2 km) and can take over 10 hours.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Aldeanueva De Barbarroya?
A: The region features a mix of open countryside and areas with natural vegetation, characterized by rolling hills and significant elevation changes, especially towards the Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda. Expect ascents and descents, providing a challenging environment.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025309331/comarca-de-la-jara-circular-to-sierra-ancha-and-sierra-aguda-from-aldeanueva-de-barbarroya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Comarca de la Jara – circular to Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya&lt;/a&gt;, offering a circular path through the Comarca de la Jara.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in terms of landmarks or points of interest?
A: Cyclists can encounter various points of interest. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3851007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amador Bridge or Azután Viaduct&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5417642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Azután Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2552420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Arzobispo&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2683470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcaudete de la Jara Town Square and Monuments&lt;/a&gt; also offer a cultural stop.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Aldeanueva De Barbarroya?
A: Considering the region's warm climate, especially in summer, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling. The milder temperatures during these seasons make tackling the rolling hills and ascents more enjoyable.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Aldeanueva De Barbarroya?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied topography, the challenge of the ascents and descents, and the scenic views of the Sierra Ancha and Sierra Aguda.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging routes for advanced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133657" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledo del Mazo – Los Navalucillos loop from Estación de Silos&lt;/a&gt;, with its substantial elevation gain, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aldeanueva de Barbarroya – Aldeanueva de Barbarroya loop from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya&lt;/a&gt;, which also features significant climbing, are well-suited for experienced road cyclists seeking a demanding ride.

Q: Where do road cycling routes typically start in Aldeanueva De Barbarroya?
A: Many routes either start directly from Aldeanueva de Barbarroya or from nearby points like Estación de Silos, making them accessible for local and visiting cyclists.
tips: Built at the end of the 14th century, the bridge has given its name to the town on the other side of the Tagus. It plays a fundamental role in communication with Extremadura, Ávila and Lisbon. Going a little further along the banks of the river, you will have some beautiful views of the bridge with the town behind.

La Nava de Ricomalilo is a small town in the region of La Jara surrounded by a beautiful countryside of olive groves and private orchards at the foot of a hill called the Risco de La Palma. The environment is very pleasant and in its urban center is the curious stone and brick tower of the town hall. The town has various services ideal for a stop on the way.

Much has changed in Aldeanueva de Barbaroya since the train stopped passing through here, although the Vía Verde de la Jara now passes through its tracks, attracting other types of visitors. However, this road does not cross the town either, so perhaps you can enjoy it more if you come with a road bike through the lonely roads that reach it and stop for a few minutes to observe its popular architecture that tells you so much about geology and history From this place.

waypoints.name: Town Hall Clock Tower of La Nava de Ricomalillo
